title: Quick Search shows origin suggestions after typing a partial query
description: Verifies that an authenticated user can open quick-search and see autocomplete suggestions after typing a partial origin query.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search
Type "par" into the origin IATA input
Verify the origin suggestions dropdown is visible and contains airport suggestion items

title: Quick Search selects an origin suggestion with the mouse
description: Verifies that clicking a visible origin suggestion fills the origin field with an IATA code and closes the suggestions list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search and wait until the auth guard has finished and input[name="origin_iata"] is visible
Type "par" into input[name="origin_iata"] and wait for #origin-suggestions to become visible
Click the first visible button inside #origin-suggestions
Verify input[name="origin_iata"] now contains a 3-letter IATA code and #origin-suggestions is no longer visible

title: Quick Search supports keyboard selection in origin autocomplete
description: Verifies that ArrowDown and Enter can select an origin suggestion from the autocomplete list.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search and wait until the auth guard has finished and input[name="origin_iata"] is visible
Type "par" into input[name="origin_iata"] and wait for #origin-suggestions to become visible
Focus input[name="origin_iata"], press ArrowDown once, then press Enter once while #origin-suggestions is visible
Verify input[name="origin_iata"] contains a selected 3-letter IATA code after those key presses and #origin-suggestions is no longer visible

title: Quick Search hides suggestions when the origin input is cleared
description: Verifies that clearing the origin input removes the visible suggestions dropdown.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search and wait until the auth guard has finished and input[name="origin_iata"] is visible
Type "par" into input[name="origin_iata"] and wait for #origin-suggestions to become visible
Verify #origin-suggestions is visible
Clear input[name="origin_iata"]
Verify #origin-suggestions is not visible

title: Quick Search blocks empty route submission with validation feedback
description: Verifies that submitting quick-search without origin and destination shows a visible validation error and stays on the quick-search page.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search and wait until the auth guard has finished and both route inputs are visible
Clear input[name="origin_iata"] and input[name="destination_iata"] if they contain any values, and ensure the route fields remain empty before submitting
Focus input[name="origin_iata"] and press Enter once to submit the empty route state without selecting any date or route suggestion

Verify the page stays on /quick-search, a visible validation message includes the current copy that starts with 'Please enter a search', and the visible inline validation uses current quick-search elements instead of inferred XPath

title: Quick Search accepts a valid route and outbound date without inline validation errors
description: Verifies that after selecting valid origin and destination values and an outbound date, the search can be submitted without inline validation errors.

steps.description: Navigate to /login
Type "{{LOGIN_USER}}" into the email field
Type "{{LOGIN_PASSWORD}}" into the password field
Click the "Iniciar sesión" button
Navigate to /quick-search and wait until the auth guard has finished and both route inputs plus the date picker trigger are visible
Type "par" into input[name="origin_iata"], wait for #origin-suggestions, and click the first visible button inside #origin-suggestions
Type "dub" into input[name="destination_iata"], wait for #destination-suggestions, and click the first visible button inside #destination-suggestions
Open the outbound date picker, choose the first enabled future date, and close the date picker if it stays open
Click button[type="submit"] only after both route inputs contain 3-letter IATA codes and the outbound date trigger shows a selected date
Verify there are no visible .qs-error elements for origin, destination, or outbound date after submission, and do not treat a mere URL check as sufficient proof

title: Quick Search filter console opens coverage controls and applies nearby radius
description: Verifies the guided filter console can open the advanced drawer, apply nearby-airport coverage, and expose active chips before a search.

steps.description: Navigate to /login, authenticate, then navigate to /quick-search and wait for input[name="origin_iata"] plus [data-ui="qs-filter-console"] to be visible
Click [data-ui="qs-filter-open"]
Verify [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"] is visible and contains the Coverage section

Click [data-ui="qs-filter-card-coverage"] if the drawer is not already open, then click the Regional preset or set [data-ui="qs-filter-radius-input"] to 250 after enabling nearby origin and destination
Close the drawer
Verify [data-ui="qs-filter-active-chips"] is visible and contains nearby/radius chips, and [data-ui="qs-filter-count"] shows a non-zero active filter count

title: Quick Search runs MAD-DUB with coverage and time filters
description: Verifies a MAD-DUB search can run with nearby coverage, a departure time window, and strict mode visible in the console.

steps.description: Authenticate, navigate to /quick-search, set input[name="origin_iata"] to MAD and input[name="destination_iata"] to DUB, and select the first enabled outbound date

Open [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"], enable [data-ui="qs-filter-nearby-origin"] and [data-ui="qs-filter-nearby-destination"], set [data-ui="qs-filter-radius-input"] to 150, set [data-ui="qs-filter-depart-after"] to 06:00 and [data-ui="qs-filter-depart-before"] to 22:00, then close the drawer
Click button[type="submit"] and wait for loading to finish or for a provider/degraded state to appear
Verify the page shows results, an empty state, or a provider error state, and the submitted UI still shows the active coverage/time filter chips without inline validation errors

title: Quick Search applies visible-result filters after AGP-DUB search
description: Verifies client-side result filters for price, duration, risk, and sort remain usable after searching AGP-DUB.

steps.description: Authenticate, navigate to /quick-search, search AGP to DUB with the first enabled outbound date, and wait for results or a final state

Open [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"], set [data-ui="qs-filter-price-max"] to 120, set [data-ui="qs-filter-duration-max"] to 240, set [data-ui="qs-filter-risk"] to low, set [data-ui="qs-filter-sort"] to price, then close the drawer
Verify [data-ui="qs-filter-active-chips"] includes price, duration, and risk filters; if results exist, verify the visible result count updates without a new validation error

title: Quick Search handles LEI-DUB exclusions and reset
description: Verifies exclusions accept valid IATA chips, reject invalid tokens, and reset correctly for a LEI-DUB scenario.

steps.description: Authenticate, navigate to /quick-search, set origin LEI and destination DUB, and select a future outbound date
Open [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"], type MAD INVALID BCN into [data-ui="qs-filter-exclude-origins"], type DUB LIS into [data-ui="qs-filter-exclude-destinations"], and blur each field
Verify valid IATA chips such as MAD, BCN, DUB, and LIS are shown while INVALID is not accepted as a chip
Click Restablecer grupo in the Coverage section
Verify exclusion chips are removed and coverage active chips are cleared

title: Quick Search marks self-connect filters as partial support
description: Verifies the self-connect controls are visually separated as partial support and expose buffer/max-stops controls.

steps.description: Authenticate, navigate to /quick-search, open [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"], and scroll to [data-ui="qs-filter-experimental"]
Verify the section contains the partial-support label and the self-connect warning copy
Enable [data-ui="qs-filter-include-stops"], set [data-ui="qs-filter-max-stops"] to 2, and set [data-ui="qs-filter-buffer-min"] to 60
Verify active chips or section summary indicate self-connect/buffer filters are active

title: Quick Search reset all clears active guided-console filters
description: Verifies the global reset-all action clears active coverage, timing, visible-result, and self-connect filters using stable selectors.

steps.description: Authenticate, navigate directly to /quick-search, and wait until input[name="origin_iata"], input[name="destination_iata"], and [data-ui="qs-filter-console"] are visible
Click [data-ui="qs-filter-open"] and wait for [data-ui="qs-filter-drawer"] to be visible

Click [data-ui="qs-filter-preset-regional"], set [data-ui="qs-filter-depart-after"] to 09:00, set [data-ui="qs-filter-price-max"] to 120, click [data-ui="qs-filter-include-stops"], and set [data-ui="qs-filter-buffer-min"] to 60
Close the drawer with Escape so the console summary and active chips are visible
Verify [data-ui="qs-filter-active-chips"] contains active chips and [data-ui="qs-filter-count"] reports a non-zero active filter count
Click [data-ui="qs-filter-reset-all"] in the console active-chip area
Verify [data-ui="qs-filter-active-chips"] no longer contains chip buttons and the console returns to the no-extra-filters copy
